title: Macroptilium martii
taxonomy: Macroptilium martii is a member of the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Phaseoleae. Macroptilium martii is a species of flowering plant in the genus Macroptilium. The plant is native to Central and South America. It is a perennial or annual herb with a wide distribution across the Americas.

intro: Macroptilium martii is a perennial legume plant native to Central America, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower cholesterol levels and alleviate symptoms of diabetes, making it a potential natural remedy for cardiovascular health and metabolic disorders. The bioactive constituents of Macroptilium martii include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
